name: Springerle Cookie Mix (from Germany)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour and baking soda.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs with an electric mixer until light.
3. Gradually beat in powdered sugar; continue beating on high speed for about 15 minutes or until the mixture resembles soft meringue.
4. Stir in anise oil.
5. Add approximately three-quarters of the flour mixture and beat on low speed until combined.
6. By hand, stir in the remaining flour mixture. Cover; let stand about 15 minutes.
7. Divide dough into thirds. On a lightly floured surface, roll each portion into an 8-inch square.
8. Let stand for 1 minute.
9. Dust a springerle (little horse) mold or rolling pin with additional flour.
10. Press the mold on the dough hard enough to make a clear design; repeat for the remaining dough (roll the springerle rolling pin on the dough, pressing hard enough to make a clear design).
11. With a sharp knife, cut the rolled cookies apart.
12. Place on a lightly floured surface; cover with a towel and let stand about 6 hours or overnight.
13. Sprinkle a greased cookie sheet with crushed aniseed.
14. Brush excess flour from the cookies with your finger.
15. Place on prepared cookie sheet.
16. Bake in a 300-degree oven for about 18 minutes, or until a light straw color. Remove to a wire rack; cool.
